_notes: 10-round baseline (v3) of TurnRDV2_alfworld_SOTA_8round_mlpr32. Differences vs v2 (the parallel 8-round fresh run): (a) rounds=10 (intended geometry; orchestrator must launch with --rounds 10), (b) turnrd.recency_decay_half_life=4.0 enables soft per-batch decay so old replay rows are downweighted rather than physically trimmed. Each batch's loss is scaled by mean(0.5 ** ((max_round_idx - round_idx) / 4)), so a row from R0 contributes weight ~0.21 at R9's train_turnrd, ~0.5 at R4's, and ~1.0 at R0's own train_turnrd. Legacy rows (no round_idx — would only appear if the operator pre-seeds the buffer with an older replay) get a fixed weight 0.5. min_batch_weight=1e-3 floors the per-batch multiplier so pure-stale batches still contribute a tiny gradient. (c) env.env_kwargs.config.dataset.num_train_games bumped 200 → 400 so the 800 H-GRPO episodes (10×80) cycle each game ~2× instead of ~4× — counters the saturation contributor identified after v1's late-round dip while keeping the env-pool construction cost manageable (~+5-10 s per Modal container). (d) seed=31 picks a task-id slice [24800, 25600) for rounds=10/eps=80 that is disjoint from v1 (seed=11 -> [5280, 5760)), v2 (seed=23 -> [14720, 15360)), and eval ([6500, 6600)). (e) turnrd.lambda_value bumped 0.5 → 1.0: doubles the weight on the per-turn V-head loss vs the R-prediction + ranking + progress-prior losses. Justification: AlfWorld populates a dense per-turn `progress_signal` via the alfworld_adapter's intermediate_reward reconciliation chain (TextWorld native → facts_diff → expert-plan fallback). With use_facts_diff_intermediate_reward=true on this run, facts-diff fires for every step that newly satisfies a PDDL fluent (often denser than expert-plan-length). The V-head is what drives α-weights via vProjection, so a stronger V-head fit propagates downstream to better credit-assignment. (f) Launcher default TURNRD_EPOCHS bumped 3 → 5: more gradient steps per train_turnrd round. Combined with recency-decay (which reduces the effective dataset size since older rows contribute less), extra epochs ensure the V-head still receives a strong fit signal per round. Adds ~30 s × 10 = ~5 min wall-time. (g) Deliberately NOT enabled: hgpo.lambda_consistency stays at 0.0 (all TurnRDv2 configs keep it off — vProjection already enforces sum-to-R structurally; the consistency loss would mostly regularize per-position normalization noise). Same arch (rank-32 + MLP targets, vProjection, alpha=0.5) and same SFT warm-start so the experimental variables vs v2 are: rounds, decay, num_train_games, lambda_value, TURNRD_EPOCHS.
